Title: Change the Hostname of a Linux Computer
Description: Permanently change the hostname of a Linux machine using hostnamectl

Sections.Content:
## Change the Linux Hostname
The hostname identifies your machine on the network. Changing it requires updating /etc/hostname and /etc/hosts.

### Step 2: Set the new hostname
This sets it permanently (survives reboots).

### Step 3: Update /etc/hosts to reflect the new hostname
Find the line with the old hostname and update it.

In /etc/hosts, change the line:
127.0.1.1   OLD_HOSTNAME
to:
127.0.1.1   $variable:NewHostname$
### Step 5 (Optional): Reboot to fully apply the change
